name: Database Failures Followed by User Changes
notes: Reports when there are failures followed by the addition or change of a user account.
bb_references: BB:Database: User Addition or Change in any order with the same IP from the previous source
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:HostDefinition: Database Servers, BB:HostReference: Database Servers

when at least 2 of these BB:Database: System Action Deny, in any order, with the same destination IP followed by at least 1 of these BB:Database: User Addition or Change in any order with the same IP from the previous source, within 5 minutes
thresholds: when at least 2 of these BB:Database: System Action Deny, in any order, with the same destination IP followed by at least 1 of these BB:Database: User Addition or Change in any order with the same IP from the previous source, within 5 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Database Failures Followed by User Changes on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:HostDefinition: Database Servers, BB:HostReference: Database Servers

AND when at least 2 of these BB:Database: System Action Deny, in any order, with the same destination IP followed by at least 1 of these BB:Database: User Addition or Change in any order with the same IP from the previous source, within 5 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Multiple Failures Followed by User Changes
Event description: Reports when there are failures followed by the addition or change of a user account.  This could mean an attacker could be increasing their privileges.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
